items.title: When Does Muon Help Agentic Reinforcement Learning?
items.summary: Muon is competitive with AdamW in large-scale pre-training, but its value for reinforcement-learning (RL) post-training remains unclear. We study vanilla Muon in sparse-reward agentic RL through matched single-seed comparisons with AdamW on ALFWorld using Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct. Under Group-in-Group

items.title: An Exam for Active Observers

items.title: When Do Multi-Agent Systems Help? An Information Bottleneck Perspective
items.summary: LLM powered multi-agent systems (MAS) have emerged as a promising paradigm for complex tasks. items.title: A Methodology for Auditable Trustworthiness Levels in AI Lifecycle Governance

items.title: Understanding Reasoning from Pretraining to Post-Training

items.title: DADiff: Diffusion-Driven Cross-Domain Policy Adaptation for Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Transferring policies across domains poses a vital challenge in reinforcement learning, due to the dynamics mismatch between the source and target domains. items.title: Loop the Loopies!
items.summary: We present Loopie, the most powerful looped Transformer to date. The Loopie series consists of two Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: a 20B-parameter model with 2B active parameters and a 6Bparameter model with 0.6B active parameters. items.title: SciForge: An AI-Native, Multimodal Workbench for Scientific Discovery

items.title: Revisiting data-driven dynamic security assessment with a tabular foundation model

items.title: Rethinking Quantum Continual Learning with Quantum Fisher Information
items.summary: Quantum continual learning aims to train quantum models on sequential tasks without losing previously learned knowledge. items.title: PagedWeight: Efficient MoE LLM Serving with Dynamic Quality-Aware Weight Quantization

items.title: A Blueprint for Equilibrium-Based Differentiable Continuous-Variable Thermodynamic Computing

items.title: Behaviour-Conditioned Neural Processes for Adaptive Residential Short-Term Load Forecasting

items.title: Learning Standard Model structure from LHC data with Riemannian flow matching
items.summary: In this work we demonstrate that a single transformer-based generative model can capture Standard Model structure spanning five decades of invariant mass, from the sub-GeV regime to the TeV continuum, a range that no single Monte Carlo sample covers.
